observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to:

Coordinate with the different national agencies to come up with appropriate action regarding the unutilized balance of the fund transfer. Return of excess funds shall be done immediately if the other party requires such action, otherwise, reclassify the amount involved to its proper account;

Observe strict compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013, and the requirements and intent in the Approved Memorandum of Agreement and monitor timely liquidation of expenditures/disbursements; and, 

Prepare adjusting entries to correct prior period errors.
observations.managementAction:
Will locate the source agency in Manila to resubmit the liquidation reports. Need to visit the source agency in Manila or at least have their contact number to resubmit the liquidation reports (P229,932.69)


Need to visit the source agency in Manila or atleast have their contact number to clarify issues regarding any balances. Management will correct once the management coordinated and reconciled with DAR.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:

Enjoin the Inventory Committee to expedite the appraisal of all unserviceable properties; and,
Create a Disposal Committee which will oversee the actual disposal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still in the process of conducting the ocular inspections of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to GAD policies to implement GAD- related activities, properly coordinate with all the personnel/offices involved, and ensure proper monitoring of the GAD PPAs, to be able to implement all GAD PPAs as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS will conduct gender analysis to come up with the data base as reference to effective prioritization of PPAs and to ensure the effective planning and utilization of GAD fund.
observations.reason:
Some PPAs were not implemented during the year. 

Also, the GAD implementers mostly fully implemented their PPAs based on the physical performance but some requested amount (Budget) were overly estimated compared to their actual needs, hence, under utilization in the financial performance

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:

Enjoin the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to expedite the review and revision of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with the requirements of the law and have the same approved by the Environmental Management Bureau;

Appropriate a sufficient amount for the purchase of a new lot, environmental assessment, geo-sensitivity test, and other relevant tests necessary for the proposed landfill; and

Ensure the sustainability, viability, effectiveness, and relevance of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
WACS has already been conducted and assisted by EMB Region V however there are still barangays that have not participated in the conduct of WACS due to pending of data analysis which are needed in the finalization of Solid Waste Management Plan

The municipality have already allocated an amount (P1,200,000.00) for the purchase of new site for the proposed Solid Waste Management Site and or Land Fill Site and said lot has been already assessed by the EMB however, the prospect lot seller has backed out.

The remaining barangays were advised to conduct WACS with the assistance of MENRO office within March 2024.

There is a new location at Barangay Cabasag which was recently assessed by EMB V. The Municipal government will realign the said budget to the new prospect location.
observations.reason: WACS has already been conducted and assisted by EMB Region V however there are still barangays that have not participated in the conduct of WACS due to pending of data analysis which are needed in the finalization of Solid Waste Management Plan

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to obtain a certificate of pending liquidation or distribution sheet from end-user units during the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: The management will fully comply with the recommendation and will ensure that Year end physical count reports reconcile with Inventory records.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
direct the accountable officers to submit the lacking supporting documents to their request for relief from accountability so that the amount of ₱430,000.00 representing the cost of the 40 dead breeding stocks could be appropriately dropped from the books of accounts; and

to conduct investigation on the matter to establish whether or not negligence of accountable officers occurred in the process and/or if such merits appropriate sanctions.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the 16 DVs amounting to ₱80,751.93 and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered.
observations.managementAction: The former employee is still processing her clearance to deduct the disallowance notice connected to this recomendation

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
Create a Disposal Committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable furniture and fixtures and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items and drop these unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal;

Assign personnel to locate/account the missing or non-existing items noted during the inventory taking or trace the documents of previous years to serve as reference to identify the accountable persons for the lost properties, investigate whether there is a need to demand settlement from the accountable persons and/or submit request for relief from accountability; and

if warranted, facilitate the process of derecognition following the guidelines and provisions of applicable COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: The disposal process is still on-going. The disposal committee is still conducting the ocular inspection for verification of the status and existence  of all the unserviceable properties recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
For donated buildings without deeds of donation, to:

initiate the identification of the donor and document every donated asset by executing deeds of donation duly signed by the donor and donee reflecting thereon the type of asset being donated, the related costs, the intent for the use of the said donation and furnish the acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant copies thereof for recording purposes;

direct both the Treasurer and the Accountant to record all donated assets in the books based on the deeds of donations received.
observations.managementAction:
The donors cannot execute deed of donation due to their lack of records.

The deed of donation required as basis for recording are not yet secured. | Follow up was done by the Municipal Assessor’s office but donors cannot execute the deed of donation due to their lack of records.

observations.recommendation:

Confirm the status of the aforementioned cash balances from the Source Agencies and the legal basis for maintaining the same in the Special Trust Fund accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The management could not trace the source agency for the following reasons:

The previous MT failed to obtain a CM copy from the LBP and the latter cannot anymore retrieve the needed information in their database.
Some balances were already in the SL prior to CYs 2009 and 2011

The management was able to return to the source agency the total amount of P316,123.69 in January 2023.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
Enjoin the concerned office to exert effort to retrieve the MOAs covering the fund transfers to NGAs, GOCCs and LGUs and also confirm with the Implementing Agencies existence of the said fund transfer; and

Request for write-off of the dormant receivable accounts following the guidelines prescribe in COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
The management is still in the process of complying with the list of required documentation for the said request.

The management is still in the process of complying with the list of required documentation for the said request.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Allot/ reserve a storage room or area to keep all purchased and available
supplies and inventory.
b. Require the responsible office/ officer to submit the RIS and liquidation
reports or distribution list to enable the recording of expense accounts.
c. Update the Physical Count of Agricultural Supplies for Distribution,
Merchandise Inventory, Other Supplies and Materials, and Accountable
forms to assert the existence, completeness and correctness of the asset.
d. Discontinue the self-review system and ensure segregation of duties and
other relevant internal controls pertinent to the monitoring and control of
supplies and other commodities.
e. Maintain the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Card to effectively monitor
the receipt and release of inventory.
f. Conduct semi-annual physical count and timely submit complete RPCI to
the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Liquidate fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund
the unutilized amount from completed projects and require the appropriate
recording in the books of accounts. Return of excess funds shall be done
immediately if the other party requires such action, otherwise reclassify the
amount involved to its proper account;
b. Trace records, prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the balance of
₱150,000.00 as of December 31, 2024, otherwise, revert the amount to the
General Fund account; and,
c. Observe strict compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013, and the
requirements and intent in the Approved Memorandum of Agreement and
monitor timely liquidation of expenditures/disbursements.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Revisit GAD related laws and regulations such as PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memo Circular 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memo Circular 2016-01 and strictly adhere to the required policies in
preparing/formulating the Annual GPB.
b. Enjoin the GFPS to formulate Gender Agenda and conduct Gender Analysis.
Henceforth, update Agency’s Gender Database.
c. Properly coordinate with all the personnel/offices involved, and ensure proper
monitoring of the GAD PPAs, to be able to implement all GAD PPAs as
planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Enjoin the contractor and other accountable officials to perform corrective
measures to ensure compliance with the approved design and technical
specifications.
b. Ensure strict adherence to RA 9184 and other pertinent laws.
c. Enjoin the Municipal Engineering Office to effectively perform its duties
and responsibilities in conducting proper evaluation, assessment and
planning of projects prior to its implementation to warrant effective
preparation of designs and specifications and to fully achieve the purpose of
the appropriations.

For the noted deficiency, we recommended that Management:
a. Fully implement all projects embodied in the annual budget/appropriation
ordinance and funded under the 20% Local Development Fund during the year
the projects are planned to be executed to ensure that the constituents of the
Barangay can immediately benefit from the projects.
b. Justify to the Audit Team the causes of the non-implementation or partial
implementation of the PPAs under 20% Economic Development Fund.

We reiterated that Management:
a. Enjoin the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to expedite the
review and revision of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan in
accordance with the requirements of the law and have the same approved
by the Environmental Management Bureau;
b. Require implementation of solid waste segregation at the source to avoid
potential fines and penalties while ensuring environmental protection and
public health.
c. Formulate solutions to address the increasing volume of the solid waste in
the Municipality and to ensure effective and efficient operation of the MRF
and achievement of its objectives.
d. Strictly implement ordinances relevant to proper Solid Waste Management.
e. Educate and inform constituents including Agency personnel of the
necessity of proper Solid Waste Management.

We reiterated that the Management:
a. Inform the Audit Team Leader within ten days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings
of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the audit team; and
b. Ensure that project details are properly indicated in all the signages unless
otherwise, such details will pose a threat to the implementation of the PPA.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management enjoin the:

Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to post on the PhilGEPS website all procurement opportunities, results of bidding, and other related information including the RFQ, NOA, and NTP for the procurements done through public bidding and all instances of alternative methods of procurements, except for contracts with ABC of ₱50,000.00 and below; and,

Municipal Accountant to ensure that the proof of postings on the PhilGEPS website is attached to the Disbursement Vouchers.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:

Strictly observe the installation of signboards/billboards within the project’s location in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability and encourage citizen participation;

Inform the Audit Team Leader within ten  days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the audit team; and,

Ensure that a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year are submitted to the audit team at the beginning of the year, and provided with complete information as indicated in Section 2.1 of COA Circular 2013-004.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20 per cent EDF to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the PPAs were implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that Management enjoin the LDRRM Council to ensure the optimum utilization of its LDRRM Plan and appropriate sufficient funds for every    pre-disaster activity so as not to deprive their constituents of the benefits that would be derived from these pre-disaster activities. And, if necessary, reprogram redundant activities to other pre-disaster PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Majority of the items programmed for 2023 was intended for calamity/disaster response which were not used for a reason that there was no calamity to respond then. Some were not used but activities were completed using funds from other sources.
Most of the Pre-disaster preparedness PPAs were implemented in 2024 but still the agency is trying their best to fully maximize the utilization of fund to fully comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the 16 DVs amounting to ₱80,751.93 and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested from concerned former employee to allow the deduction of the disallowed amount from her terminal leave pay.
observations.reason: The former employee is yet to process her clearance to claim said terminal leave pay.

The inventory report was not yet prepared.




Inventory Report from the MAO is not yet submitted to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Strictly require the end-user unit to submit the distribution sheet or
liquidation report of all inventory items used or distributed as of year-end;
and
b. Enjoin the Inventory Committee to obtain a certificate of pending
liquidation or distribution sheet from end-user units during the actual
physical count.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Treasurer
to:
a. Observe strict compliance with Section 271 (d) of the R.A. No. 7160 on the
release of RPT shares in order for the barangays to utilize their funds timely
for their priority PPAs; and
b. Cause the immediate release of the balance of unremitted RPT shares for
the respective barangays.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Expedite the preparation and approval of the Solid Waste Management
Plan;
b. Submit proposals for funding support from the Solid Waste Management
Fund; and
c. Ensure that the revisions and comments provided by the EMB Regional
Office are noted and effected.

We recommended and Management agreed to strategically plan the
implementation of the projects to ensure that all projects funded under the 20% LDF
are at least completed in the year they are intended to be implemented, and the benefits
that could have been derived therefrom are realized/enjoyed by the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Demand from the concerned suppliers the payment of liquidated
damages/penalties, or impose and deduct the same from any existing claims
of said suppliers; and
b. Exercise sound management in the proper review of the terms and
conditions of contracts that the LGU seeks to engage in.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the MEO to:
a. Conduct the field survey and investigation satisfactorily and efficiently to
prepare accurate design and production plans; and
b. Follow the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in
relation to contract implementation guidelines for the procurement of
infrastructure projects in cases where changes or modifications in the
original design plan and POW are warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Enjoin the concerned office to exert efforts to retrieve the MOAs covering the fund transfers to NGAs, GOCCs and LGUs and also confirm with the Implementing Agencies existence of the said fund transfer; and
Request for write-off of the dormant receivable accounts following the guidelines prescribe in COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that Management enjoin:
The MAO and GSO to further investigate the unaccounted inventory items and submit the result of investigation with accompanying documents to serve as basis for the Municipal Accountant to be able to reconcile the discrepancies between RPCI and the Books of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Create a Disposal Committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable furniture and fixtures and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items and drop these unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal;
Assign personnel to locate/account the missing or non-existing items noted during the inventory taking or trace the documents of previous years to serve as reference to identify the accountable persons for the lost properties, investigate whether there is a need to demand settlement from the accountable persons and/or submit request for relief from accountability; and
If warranted, facilitate the process of disposition and derecognition process following the guidelines and provisions of applicable COA Circulars.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Implement the programs/projects using the funds received from various agencies;
Secure electronic copies and maintain database of all Memorandum of Agreement or Contracts with National Agencies for a more effective record keeping; and
Confirm the status of the aforementioned cash balances from the Source Agencies and the legal basis for maintaining the same in the Special Trust Fund accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to enjoin:
The MAO to prepare the inventory on Biological Assets together with documents relative to additions, distributions, reproduction, and death of the breeding stocks at the end of the year and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy; and
The Municipal Accountant to recognize the biological assets and agricultural produce that satisfy the recognition criteria including any gain or loss arising from biological transformation. If not, then disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements items on biological assets and agricultural produce required under PPSAS 27.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the MAO to submit to the Municipal Accountant the available documents which may serve as evidence for proper recording of the transactions.
Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance and guidelines providing the measures to generate local revenue from the sale of any agricultural produce from the municipal farm under the management of the MAO.

We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with GAD policies to implement GAD related activities and institute proper planning of GAD PPAs to avoid delay in the implementation.

As to the various debit memos, make an investigation on whether there was negligence on the part of accountable officer during the time these transactions occurred and if warranted, recommend appropriate sanction; and 












As to the unsubmitted paid disbursement vouchers, demand the immediate settlement of ₱80,751.93 from the accountable officer.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has exerted efforts to obtain the required Debit Memos, however it is no longer possible to rectify the deficiency. The Management found out that there was negligence but no proceeds if the said DMs went to personal pocket of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

The necessary adjustments in the books had been made based on the recommendation of the Local Finance Committee. (JEV No. 101-2021-04-1148C dated April 30, 2021)

Necessary entry has been made by the Municipal Accountant to take up the disallowance charges on April 30, 2021 under JEV No. 101-2021-04-1148B and 401-2021-04-117.

observations.recommendation:
Ensure that the limitation/conditions set forth in the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta to PHWs prescribed under DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2012 are strictly followed.

Review the Daily Time Record (DTR) of PHWs before computing and granting the allowance.
observations.managementAction: The Management revised its procedure in the computation and granting of the said allowance and effected the computation based on the DTR duly validated and submitted by the HR taking into consideration the limitations and conditions set forth in the DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, S 2012.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Engineer or an independent professional possessing the required education or experience regarding the matter to make a thorough investigation of the incident of foundation settlement at a depth of 0.50 meters of the structure in order to determine whether the same is considered as Ruinous Buildings or Structures, if so, then proceed with the procedures as provided under Section 1.01.08 of the NBCP;


Engage the services of Structural Engineer and Geotechnical Engineer in the conduct of the Structural Analysis and Soil Analysis, respectively. The said Analyses should be the bases for the structural design/plan prepared by the Municipal Engineer.
observations.managementAction: The Management engaged the services of RPenolio Consulting Engineers to evaluate the status of the project subject of the observation. The Technical Report on such engagement found that the structure is not ruinous and thus mitigation and retrofitting could restore it to its original position. The Structural Analysis also showed that there is adequate support for the anticipated loads that could be imparted on the structure.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Disposal Committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable furniture and fixtures and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items and to  be able to drop these unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal; and 

Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to exert effort to locate/account the missing or non-existing items noted during the inventory taking or trace the documents of previous years to serve as reference to identify the accountable persons for the lost properties, and require them to settle their accountabilities and/or submit the request for relief from accountability, if necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the 16 DVs amounting to ₱80,751.93 and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:
As to the various debit memos, make an investigation on whether there was negligence on the part of accountable officer during the time these transactions occurred and if warranted, recommend appropriate sanction; and
As to the unsubmitted paid disbursement vouchers, demand the immediate settlement of ₱80,751.93 from the accountable officer.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the List of Real Property Tax Receivable for CY 2020 to the Municipal Accountant and in the ensuing years, furnish the same to the Accountant at the beginnning of every year; and
require the Municipal Accountant to record the corresponding RPT and SET Receivables based from the report or list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the end-user units to prepare the Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) on all the inventories issued to them for approval by the GSO; and
Require the GSO to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS and submit to the Accounting Unit for the preparation of JEV to recognize the issuances in the expense account and for the reconciliation with the Inventory Items balance per book.

We recommended that the Management institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Create a Disposal Committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable furniture and fixtures and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items and drop these unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal;
Assign personnel to locate/account the missing or non-existing items noted during the inventory taking or trace the documents of previous years to serve as reference to identify the accountable persons for the lost properties, investigate whether there is a need to demand settlement from the accountable persons and/or submit request for relief from accountability; and
if warranted, facilitate the process of derecognition following the guidelines and provisions of applicable COA Circulars.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Ensure that the limitation/conditions set forth in the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta to PHWs prescribed under DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2012 are strictly followed.
Review the Daily Time Record (DTR) of PHWs before computing and  granting the allowance.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

ensure that the amount of Credit Memoranda (CM) totaling ₱340,534.00 are recorded and initially recognized as liability until such time that adequate documentation would show that said CM are appropriately owned by the LGU; and


in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, request the depository bank to waive the bank service charges amounting to ₱1,200.00 deducted from their two (2) trust fund accounts as these are disallowable in audit.
observations.managementAction:
A detailed list of requested DMs was submitted to LBP Naga Branch for said branch to coordinate and request for these documents at their main office.


Requested LBP Naga Branch for the reversion of the bank service charges.
observations.reason:
LBP failed to furnish management with requested DMs despite several follow ups. LBP claimed that their records for said DMs are no longer available at their main office.


LBP, Naga Branch gave assurance that they will request their main office to revert the amount to our depository bank account in July 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to: 

require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Property Officer and other officials concerned, to gather all the details and information needed to determine the particulars/specifications of each item recorded in the RPIs, and 



direct the Municipal Accountant to thereafter, take up the necessary journal entries to recognize in the books the public infrastructure assets totaling ₱27,711,928.59, as well as the related accumulated depreciation and impairment losses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004, to present fairly the value of the assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant shall continue the tracing of remaining DVs covering payments of public infrastructures in prior years. Necessary journal entries to recognize payments of public infrastructure assets shall be prepared based on the DVs and supporting documents.

A composite Inventory Team has been organized, which shall take charge of the actual physical inventory verification of the unrecorded public infrastructure assets as of December 31, 2019.

The Municipal Engineer shall prepare a Schedule of Estimated Useful Life of Public Infrastructure Assets which shall be the basis of recognizing the related accumulated depreciation and impairment losses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

direct the accountable officers to submit the lacking supporting documents to their request for relief from accountability so that the amount of ₱430,000.00 representing the cost of the 40 dead breeding stocks could be appropriately dropped from the books of accounts and 

to conduct investigation on the matter to establish whether or not negligence of accountable officers occurred in the process and/or if such merits appropriate sanctions.
observations.reason:
There are still lacking documents for other deceased animals 



No investigation is conducted yet for any negligence of accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The covering Disbursement Vouchers and the required Journal Entry Vouchers are now being processed so that the said amounts of P598,037.19 and P59, 762.54 respectively, be remitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Bureau of the Treasury and General Fund of this LGU respectively.
observations.reason: Others for implementation respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed:

for the six untitled/no transfer of title lots in the name of the LGU, to require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate their registration in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds; and 

for donated buildings without deeds of donation, to: a) initiate the identification of the donor and document every donated asset by executing deeds of donation duly signed by the donor and donee reflecting thereon the type of asset being donated, the related costs, the intent for the use of the said donation and furnish the acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant copies thereof for recording purposes; b) instruct the Municipal Assessor’s Office to appraise the value of the donated assets without costs indicated in related documents, for proper recording in the books of accounts; and c) direct both the Treasurer and the Accountant to record all donated assets in the books based on the deeds of donations received.
observations.managementAction:
Three out of six untitled lots’ processing of title is on-going (Special Patent Application from CENRO).

Follow up donor to execute deed of donation
observations.reason:
Processing of titling is still on-going



No response from donors

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

observations.managementAction: The Management is conducting seminars and assemblies related to   tax information drive
observations.reason: Difficulty in complying with the requirements for issuance of building permits.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the 16 DVs and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered and/or to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.reason: Laborious retrieval of older records of RCI containing the unrecorded DVs for CYs 1994-2011.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
ensure that the amount of Credit Memoranda (CM) totaling ₱340,534.00 are recorded and initially recognized as liability until such time that adequate documentation would show that said CM are appropriately owned by the LGU; and
in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, request the depository bank to waive the bank service charges amounting to ₱1,200.00 deducted from their two (2) trust fund accounts as these are disallowable in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Disposal Committee to immediately dispose all unserviceable and unusable furniture and fixtures and equipment using the appropriate mode of disposal, as determined and recommended by the Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from the sale thereof, if warranted, make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items and to  be able to drop these unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal; and
Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to exert effort to locate/account the missing or non-existing items noted during the inventory taking or trace the documents of previous years to serve as reference to identify the accountable persons for the lost properties, and require them to settle their accountabilities and/or submit the request for relief from accountability, if necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Property Officer and other officials concerned, to gather all the details and information needed to determine the particulars/specifications of each item recorded in the RPIs.

We likewise recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to thereafter, take up the necessary journal entries to recognize in the books the public infrastructure assets totaling ₱27,711,928.59, as well as the related accumulated depreciation and impairment losses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004, to present fairly the value of the assets in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed todirect the accountable officers to submit the lacking supporting documents to their request for relief from accountability so that the amount of ₱430,000.00 representing the cost of the 40 dead breeding stocks could be appropriately dropped from the books of accounts.

We also recommended and Management agreed to conduct investigation on the matter to establish whether or not negligence of accountable officers occurred in the process and/or if such merits appropriate sanctions.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed torequire the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the 16 DVs amounting to ₱80,751.93and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered.

We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the bank and request for copies of the Debit Memos/Credit Memos, validate propriety thereof and effect the necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Management adjusted reconciling items with available data/ information such as stale checks.

Also, Management coordinated with Land Bank (Main Office) for the retrieval of the copies of CMs/DMs.
observations.reason:
Laborious retrieval of older records of RCI containing the unrecorded DVs for CYs 1994-2011.

Communication with the depository bank yielded no positive result as said depository bank was unable to retrieve copies of CMs/ DMs.

Failure of the previous Municipal Treasurer to act on these reconciling items before her retirement on August 30, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed:

for the two parcels of lots with total cost/fair market value of ₱312,736.00, to require the Municipal Accountant to take-up the same in the books and instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to update its report of the LGU’s properties;

for the six untitled/no transfer of title lots in the name of the LGU, to require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate their registration in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds; and
for donated buildings without deeds of donation, to: a) initiate the identification of the donor and document every donated asset by executing deeds of donation duly signed by the donor and donee reflecting thereon the type of asset being donated, the related costs, the intent for the use of the said donation and furnish the acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant copies thereof for recording purposes; b) instruct the Municipal Assessor’s Office to appraise the value of the donated assets without costs indicated in related documents, for proper recording in the books of accounts; and c) direct both the Treasurer and the Accountant to record all donated assets in the books based on the deeds of donations received.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant took up in the books the aforementioned two parcels of landwith valid ownership documents under JEV No. 101-19-05-1388 dated May 7, 2019 to rectify the deficiency noted in this Audit Observation.


Three out of six untitled lots’ processing of title is on-going (Special Patent Application from CENRO).

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

exert effort in getting supporting documents from the bank for the ₱3,197,733.00 credited IRA of previous year to be able to transfer said amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and

conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments which need to be recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Of the eight (8) unimplemented PPAs in 2018, Management implemented five (5) PPAs in 2019.
observations.reason:
Late release of IRA resulted to the late implementation of PPAs under the 20% Local Development Fund.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the concerned agencies and personnel to facilitate the immediate utilization of the items purchased so as not to deprive beneficiaries of the beneficial use of the tangible assets.
observations.managementAction: Project already completed and testing has been done.
The Management met with barangay officials to discuss the new rates approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to be implemented in succeeding years.
observations.reason: The Management is still familiarizing itself with the new rates and guidelines approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording obligations for purchases of goods that are not yet delivered/accepted at record date and/or without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Recorded obligations have been delivered/accepted at record date and/or complete with its documentation

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Ensure that payments are made directly to suppliers/creditors in the form of checks and only petty operating expenses should be reimbursed through the Petty Cash Fund;

Require that all procurements be properly planned depending on the available budget in order to maintain the normal flow of cash; and 

Direct the payment as obligations fall due without resorting to advances from personal funds of officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: The Petty Cash Fund Manual was prepared by the Municipal Accountant and the Petty Cash Fund under the Imprest System was established in July 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require that:

All undertakings entered into by the Municipality with  source agencies for the implementation of latter’s projects be supported with a corresponding Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and 

Specific guidelines for the project implementation and utilization of the fund be formulated to guarantee that only valid, proper and correct disbursements are undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: All BUB projects have already been implemented and funds received were liquidated with the grantor.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended and Management agreed to conduct investigation on the matter to establish whether or not negligence of accountable officers occurred in the process and/or if such merits appropriate sanctions.

observations.reason: The municipality has shifted its solid waste management from an open dumpsite to a controlled dumpsite but not yet into a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the 16 DVs and the corresponding supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions covered and/or to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.reason: Laborious retrieval of older records of RCI containing the unrecorded DVs for CYs 1994-2011. Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording obligations for purchases of goods that are not yet delivered/accepted at record date and/or without complete documentation.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Ensure that payments are made directly to suppliers/creditors in the form of checks and only petty operating expenses should be reimbursed through the Petty Cash Fund; b. Require that all procurements be properly planned depending on the available budget in order to maintain the normal flow of cash; and c. Direct the payment as obligations fall due without resorting to advances from personal funds of officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Discouraged the practice of payment through reimbursement.
observations.reason: Petty cash fund of the LGU is still to be established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to revisit the provisions of R.A. 9184 and strictly observe the RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of various goods.
observations.managementAction: Strictly followed the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require that: a. All undertakings entered into by the Municipality with source agencies for the implementation of latter’s projects be supported with a corresponding Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and b. Specific guidelines for the project implementation and utilization of the fund be formulated to guarantee that only valid, proper and correct disbursements are undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the copies of the MOA of BUB Projects

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to enforce the full implementation of all projects embodied in their Annual Budget and funded under the 20% Local Development Fund during the year the projects are planned to be executed in order that the constituents of the Municipality can immediately benefit from the projects.
observations.managementAction: Reminded concerned officers to enforce the full implementation of all projects embodied in the approved CY 2017 20% Development Fund and Budget.
observations.reason: Only one out of the three development projects was implemented as of CY 2018. Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the timely implementation of BUB and ADM projects so as not to deprive the constituents of the Municipality of the beneficial use of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Implemented the BUB projects as of CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the accountable officers concerned to request for relief from accountability so that the amount of P310,000.00 representing the cost of the 29 dead breeding stocks could be appropriately dropped from the books of accounts.

We also recommended that Management conduct investigation on the matter to establish whether or not negligence of accountable officers occurred in the process and/or if such merits appropriate sanctions.
observations.managementAction: Notice of loss and request for relief of accountability was submitted by accountable officers.
observations.reason: Investigation on reported livestock mortality has yet to be conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) start the process and implement appropriate steps that would lead to the closure of the open dump and would result to switching to sanitary landfill; b) intensify efforts to achieve and embrace the full concept of Ecological solid waste management and adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003 and their 10 Year Solid Waste Management Plan; c) discontinue the practice of collecting non-segregated waste and come up with measures that would help its cause.  Encouraging waste segregation at source will also be beneficial in transitioning from an open dumpsite to a sanitary land fill as it would lessen the amount of wastes that would go to this dumpsite; and d) adopt scheduling method/scheme which would allow the collection of different types of wastes on different days. Data on waste characterization can aid in coming up with an effective scheduling scheme.
observations.managementAction: Initial funding for the construction of MRF building/structures for the newly acquired 3.0 hec of lot at Barangay Peňafrancia.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still awaiting the completion of the MRF facilities.

observations.recommendation: For the purpose of controlling and regulating the use, operation and maintenance of motor vehicles of the Municipality, compliance with COA Circular on the use of government plate and marking of vehicles “For Official Use Only” is highly enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Management informed LTO regarding the vehicle’s transfer of plate from private to government.

Accountant to make tlie necessary adjustments in die books of accounts to refleci
the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the 20 DVs and the corresponding
supporting documents to facilitate the review and examination of the transactions
covered therein otherwise the amount of P 96,809.93 shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Maunicipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary cancellation and/or
adjustment of stale checks to restore the cash equivalent to the appropriate Cash in
Rank - LCCA accounts.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to
enforce the full implementation of all projects embedied in their Annnal Budget and
funded under the 20% Local Deveiopment Fund during the year the projects are
planied to be executed in order that the constituents of the Municipaiity can

immediately benefit from the projects.
